content.problemStatement: Swap SortYou are given an array AAA of size nnn (not exceeding 500500500), you are allowed a maximum of n‚àí1n-1n‚àí1 swaps where in each swap you can choose **any two** indices i,ji, ji,j such that 0‚â§i,j‚â§n‚àí10 \leq i, j \leq n-10‚â§i,j‚â§n‚àí1 and swap the values of AiA_iAi‚Äã and AjA_jAj‚Äã.
constraints: There may be many solutions, you can output **any** of them.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains a single integer nnn, the number of elements in the array

The second line contains nnn space separated integers, the elements of the array
content.outputFormat:
In the first line print the number of swaps you need to perform, let this number be mmm.

In the next mmm lines, print two space separated integers i,ji, ji,j such that 0‚â§i,j‚â§n‚àí10 \leq i, j \leq n-10‚â§i,j‚â§n‚àí1.

After performing the swaps in the order in which your program gives the output, the array should become sorted in increasing order.
(i.e. Ai‚â•Ai‚àí1A_i \geq A_{i-1}Ai‚Äã‚â•Ai‚àí1‚Äã for every iii in the range [1,n‚àí1][1, n-1][1,n‚àí1]).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
After the first swap, the elements A0A_0A0‚Äã and A2A_2A2‚Äã are swapped, and the array becomes sorted, i.e. {1,2,3}\{1, 2, 3\}{1,2,3}

solution.approach: **Enumeration sort** is a simple algorithm that sorts an array by finding the correct position for each element and placing it there. In this problem, we can modify it to count the swaps required and output the swap operations.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n^2), as we may need to iterate through the array up to n‚àí1 times to find and place elements.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n), due to storing the positions and the list of swaps.
